item.title: Monday, April 14 (today) — Week of 2026-04-14
item.description:
• 🟢 OpenAI Codex hitting capacity limits for users — Users reporting hitting Codex usage caps, suggesting demand is significantly outpacing current capacity allocation. Developers on Plus and Pro plans are encountering limits faster than expected on the new $100/month Pro tier.
• 📰 Spiking Neural Network scaled to 1.088B parameters from scratch — A researcher independently scaled a pure SNN (no backprop, biologically-inspired) to over 1 billion parameters and ran out of budget — findings show promising convergence and are shared as a research data point. Relevant for engineers following alternative architectures beyond transformer scaling.
• 🎥 NVIDIA's Quantum Day | here's a glimpse into the future... — **Wes Roth** — Coverage of NVIDIA's Quantum Day event, exploring how quantum computing intersects with AI acceleration — relevant for developers tracking the next wave of infrastructure that will eventually underpin AI workloads.
• 🎥 Blackbox AI vs Claude Code: Which Builds Better Websites? — **BrightN8** (7 min) — Direct head-to-head comparison of Blackbox AI and Claude Code on website-building tasks — quick signal on how a lesser-known AI coding tool stacks up against Claude Code for front-end work.
• 💬 r/ollama: "RAM guide: What model combinations actually fit on common Macs" — Practical guide showing exactly which local model combinations (e.g., two 7B models, one 30B, one 13B + one 7B) fit within Mac unified memory across M1/M2/M3/M4 configs — useful planning reference for local AI development.

item.title: Monday, April 13 (today) — Week of 2026-04-13
item.description:
• 🛠 Claude.ai outage + quality complaints trend (April 13) — Claude.ai went down briefly around 15:40 UTC today. The Register notes quality complaints have "escalated sharply" alongside the outage, with community discussion about whether Opus 4.5/4.6 has been nerfed. @lydiahallie tweeted: "We're aware people are hitting usage limits in Claude Code way faster than expected. Actively investigating."
• 🔥 Cache TTL controversy: Anthropic responds to the March 6 reversion — In early March, Anthropic silently reverted the Claude Code prompt cache TTL from 1 hour → 5 minutes, causing major cost spikes for heavy users. One developer documented $2,530 in overpayments across 119k API calls. Anthropic's public response: the 5-minute TTL is actually cheaper for "one-shot" sessions where the context is rarely re-read.
• 🛠 Model switching mid-chat now live in Claude.ai — Users can now switch between Claude models (e.g., Opus ↔ Sonnet) within an active conversation without starting a new chat. Useful for moving to a cheaper model after an expensive planning phase.
• 💜 Team signal: @trq212 notes a TurboTax connector shipped in Claude Code ("there's a turbotax connector in Claude Code now, so glad I procrastinated on taxes").
• 📰 Linux kernel now allows AI-generated code — maintainers must take "full responsibility" — A significant open-source policy shift: AI-assisted patches are now permitted in the Linux kernel, but the human submitter bears full accountability for any bugs or security issues introduced. Implications for code review workflows across the industry.

item.title: Sunday, April 13 — Week of 2026-04-07
item.description:
• 🛠 CC v2.1.94–v2.1.105: /team-onboarding, subprocess sandboxing, Monitor tool, PreCompact hook, Bedrock Mantle — A batch CHANGELOG commit landed on April 13 covering twelve versions, with major additions spanning Amazon Bedrock via Mantle, a new `/team-onboarding` command, Linux subprocess sandboxing, a `Monitor` tool for background script events, Google Vertex AI setup wizard, and a blocking `PreCompact` hook.
• 🟡 MiniMax M2.7 open-sourced: self-evolving agentic model scores 56% on SWE-Pro — MiniMax has open-sourced M2.7, their most capable model, notable for participating in its own development cycle — autonomously optimizing internal scaffolds for a 30% performance gain. It tops open-source rankings on GDPval-AA (ELO 1495) and natively supports multi-agent collaboration ("Agent Teams"). Weights on Hugging Face; runs via Ollama.
• 🔵 Google Gemini in Chrome rolling out to AI Pro/Ultra subscribers in the US — Gemini 3 arrives as a Chrome side panel for AI Pro and Ultra subscribers, with multitasking, deeper Google apps integration, and image editing via "Nano Banana." Lyria 3 Pro music generation also started full rollout to all Workspace users and personal accounts (18+) on April 8.
• 📰 Sam Altman's home targeted in drive-by shooting hours after firebomb attack — OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's residence was attacked twice in a day — widely covered in the AI developer community and raising broader questions about security for AI company leaders.
• 🎥 Building an AI Dark Factory: A Codebase That Writes Its Own Code, Live — **Cole Medin** — Live demo of a fully autonomous coding system where AI agents write, test, and ship code without human intervention — a practical look at "dark factory" agentic development using Claude Code as the backbone.

item.title: Sunday, April 12 — Week of 2026-04-06
item.description:
• 🔥 Claude Mythos Preview — Project Glasswing launches (April 7) — Anthropic announced its most capable model yet, restricted to invited defenders only. Mythos autonomously found thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across every major OS and browser during internal testing — including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug and JIT heap spray chains escaping both renderer and OS sandboxes. Considered too capable to release publicly.
• 🔥 Claude Managed Agents public beta + `ant` CLI (April 8) — Two related API launches: (1) Managed Agents is a fully managed agentic harness — create agents, configure secure sandboxed containers, and run sessions via the API using the `managed-agents-2026-04-01` beta header. Built-in tools and SSE streaming included. (2) The `ant` CLI client enables faster Claude API interaction, native CC integration, and YAML-based versioning of API resources.
• 🛠 CC v2.1.98–v2.1.101: subprocess sandboxing, Vertex wizard, team onboarding (April 9–10) — Four releases spanning the week with security and reliability focus.
• 🟡 MiniMax M2.7 — open-source with contested license — MiniMax released M2.7, their latest frontier text model, but license terms alarmed the open-source community by restricting commercial API use. MiniMax CEO Ryan Lee posted a clarification: restrictions primarily target API resellers who abused M2.5, and the license may be updated for regular users and researchers.
• 🔵 Google Gemini 3 Flash becomes the Gemini app default — Major capability upgrade (over Gemini 2.5 Flash) now shipping as the default model. Chrome side panel with Gemini 3 integration rolling out to US AI Pro/Ultra subscribers. New cross-service import tools let users bring chat history from other AI apps.
